title: RestFast Common Mistakes and Correct Usage
purpose: Prevent common coding errors and hallucinations when using RestFast

critical_mistakes.error: Calling GetDataTable() or PopulateTable() directly on Utils
critical_mistakes.wrong_code:
Dim utils As New Utils()
Dim dt As DataTable = utils.GetDataTable(si, "GetCustomers") ' ❌ WRONG
critical_mistakes.why_wrong: Utils class does NOT have GetDataTable() or PopulateTable() methods. These methods belong ONLY to RESTAPIRequest class.
critical_mistakes.correct_code:
Dim utils As New Utils()
Dim request As RESTAPIRequest = utils.GetSavedRequest(si, "GetCustomers")
Dim dt As DataTable = request.GetDataTable() ' ✅ CORRECT
critical_mistakes.rule: NEVER call GetDataTable() or PopulateTable() on Utils. Always get the RESTAPIRequest object first.

critical_mistakes.error: Passing table name as parameter to PopulateTable()
critical_mistakes.why_wrong: PopulateTable() does NOT accept table name as parameter. Table name is configured in the saved request (tableToPopulate property).
critical_mistakes.correct_code:
' Table is already configured in the saved request
request.PopulateTable(si) ' ✅ CORRECT

' Or with custom variables:
Dim vars As New Dictionary(Of String, String) From {{"key", "value"}}
request.PopulateTable(si, vars) ' ✅ CORRECT
critical_mistakes.rule: PopulateTable() signature: PopulateTable(si As SessionInfo, Optional paramVariables As Dictionary(Of String, String) = Nothing)

critical_mistakes.error: Passing request name as parameter to GetDataTable()
critical_mistakes.wrong_code: Dim dt As DataTable = utils.GetDataTable(si, "GetCustomers") ' ❌ WRONG
critical_mistakes.why_wrong: GetDataTable() is called on a RESTAPIRequest object, not Utils. It doesn't take request name as parameter.
critical_mistakes.correct_code:
Dim request As RESTAPIRequest = utils.GetSavedRequest(si, "GetCustomers")
Dim dt As DataTable = request.GetDataTable(limit:=3) ' ✅ CORRECT
critical_mistakes.rule: GetDataTable() signature: GetDataTable(Optional limit As Integer = 0, Optional paramVariables As Dictionary(Of String, String) = Nothing)

critical_mistakes.error: Confusing Utils methods with RESTAPIRequest methods
critical_mistakes.rule: Utils = helper functions to GET requests. RESTAPIRequest = execute and process requests.

correct_patterns.pattern_name: Execute a saved request and get HttpResult
correct_patterns.correct_code:
Dim utils As New Workspace.RESTAPIFramework.RESTAPIRequestManager.Utils()
Dim result As HttpResult = utils.ExecuteSavedRequest(si, "GetCustomers")
If result.StatusCode = HttpStatusCode.OK Then
    ' Process result.Content
End If
correct_patterns.when_to_use: When you want raw HTTP response to process manually

correct_patterns.pattern_name: Get DataTable from saved request with pagination
correct_patterns.correct_code:
Dim utils As New Utils()
Dim request As RESTAPIRequest = utils.GetSavedRequest(si, "GetCustomers")
Dim dt As DataTable = request.GetDataTable(limit:=3)

For Each row As DataRow In dt.Rows
    Dim id As String = row("id").ToString()
    ' Process...
Next
correct_patterns.when_to_use: When you want to process data programmatically in your Business Rule

correct_patterns.correct_code:
Dim utils As New Utils()
Dim request As RESTAPIRequest = utils.GetSavedRequest(si, "GetProducts")

' Simple - uses default variables
request.PopulateTable(si)

' Or with custom variables
Dim vars As New Dictionary(Of String, String) From {{"category", "electronics"}}
request.PopulateTable(si, vars)
correct_patterns.when_to_use: When you want to load data directly into OneStream custom table (simplest option)
correct_patterns.note: Table name must be pre-configured in the saved request (tableToPopulate property)

correct_patterns.correct_code:
Dim request As New RESTAPIRequest()
request.method = "GET"
request.endpoint = "https://api.example.com/data"
request.headers = New Dictionary(Of String, String) From {{"Accept", "application/json"}}
request.authCredentials = New BearerTokenAuthCredentials("token")

Dim result As HttpResult = request.Execute()
correct_patterns.when_to_use: When you need dynamic request configuration that can't be pre-saved

validation_rules.rule: If you see utils.GetDataTable() → WRONG, should be request.GetDataTable()
If you see utils.PopulateTable() → WRONG, should be request.PopulateTable()
If you see PopulateTable(si, requestName, tableName) → WRONG signature
validation_rules.check: PopulateTable() signature is: PopulateTable(si, Optional paramVariables)

validation_rules.rule: If you see GetDataTable(si, requestName) → WRONG signature
validation_rules.check: GetDataTable() signature is: GetDataTable(Optional limit, Optional paramVariables)
Type safety - ensure variable is RESTAPIRequest type

Utils.purpose: Helper class to retrieve and execute saved requests

RESTAPIRequest.purpose: Main class representing a REST API request configuration
RESTAPIRequest.note: Get instances using Utils.GetSavedRequest() or create with New RESTAPIRequest()

quick_reference.i_want_to: Execute a saved request and get raw response
Get a saved request object to work with
Execute request and get DataTable with data
Execute request programmatically with full control
